You’ll be drawn in to the relatable daily grind and laugh at the outrageous problem solving. And like any true clown show, the performance cannot take place without you. The egg hunt is from 8 a.m.-1p.m. and photos with Mr. Bunny is from 9 a..m.-2 p.m.

Enjoy a classic cartoon like "The Jetsons," "Flintstones," "The Banana Splits"," Ghostbusters," "Josie and the Pussycats," "TMNT," "Schoolhouse Rock" and more. address: 2708 E Cesar E Chavez Ave, Suite 101, Los Angeles, CA 90033, USA

address: 1500 N College Ave., Claremont, CA 91711, USA

Bring your friends, bring your family, and make magical springtime memories with us!
address: 6150 Laurel Canyon Boulevard, North Hollywood, CA, California 91606, USA

All food vendors have at least one $5 or less item, making tasting a variety of food accessible for all.
address: 1036 Broxton Ave., Los Angeles, CA 90024, USA

address: 111 S Grand Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90012, USA

All adoption fees are waived for the event, so it’s the perfect time to add a feline friend to your family. Book your spot ahead of time and get 25% off your Cat Lounge visit: https://www.purrrlandia.com
address: 728 N Moorpark Road, Thousand Oaks, CA 91360, USA

The community day features 30+ MENA Vendors including ceramics, art, clothes, homegoods, & more, food and drinks, cultural activations like custom Persian calligraphy, recreate your own flag and more. address: 3222 W. Jefferson Blvd, Los Angeles , CA 900018, USA

description: The National Congress of Black Women's Los Angeles Chapter hosts this special event to honor women authors. Meet beloved authors and grab their newest books. Make sure not to miss the extraordinary chance to meet them in person and exchange stories.

description: Join Leonis Adobe Museum for their annual spring celebration, just in time to see the incredible gardens in bloom and our new lambs. Enjoy a scavenger hunt with filled eggs as a prize, along with seasonal crafts, live music, 1880s-themed rancho activities and more!

title: MATTY: an evening with Christy Mathewson
description: “Matty” is the life story of Christy Mathewson, legendary hall-of-fame pitcher for the New York Giants (1900 to 1916), gentleman, philosopher and first true American hero who inspired a whole generation of fans to make baseball America's pastime. title: Hooray LA! - Sensory Friendly

As BBMT prepares for a milestone year, including their biggest stage yet at Coachella and their first new show in 40 years, Hooray LA! demonstrates how BBMT is the cultural touchstone of the city, and an artistic home of imagination and puppetry innovation for Angelenos and beyond. A sensory-friendly version of Hooray LA! will be offered on Saturday, March 28 at 1:30 p.m.
address: 4949 York Blvd., Los Angeles, CA 90042, USA

address: 8921 Beverly Blvd , Pico Rivera, CA 90201, USA

From co-founding the United Farmworkers with Cesar Chavez and Larry Itliong, to boycotts, marches, and high-stakes negotiations—her unwavering commitment to justice changed the course of history. title: National Pet Day Celebration at Vin Scully Centennial Park in La Habra
description: Vin Scully Centennial Park celebrates National Pet Day with activities honoring our four-legged friends. Families can bring their pets or simply enjoy pet-themed fun, demonstrations, and community connections. The park setting provides plenty of space for furry family members to socialize.

title: Animals Everywhere in Rancho Santa Margarita
description: Little animal lovers can meet and learn about creatures from different habitats through interactive presentations and close-up encounters. This educational program brings various animals to life through storytelling and hands-on experiences. Children leave with a deeper appreciation for wildlife and the natural world.

title: Crafty Saturday at Los Alamitos-Rossmoor Library
description: This weekly craft program at Los Alamitos-Rossmoor Library invites kids to explore different art projects and creative techniques. Each Saturday features a new hands-on activity designed to inspire young makers. Materials are provided for a fun, mess-free crafting experience.

title: Upper Newport Bay Kayak Tours ($27.27 per person)
description: Explore the serene waters of Upper Newport Bay by kayak on a guided tour perfect for active families. Paddle through one of Southern California's largest coastal wetlands while spotting local wildlife and learning about the estuary ecosystem. Tours depart regularly and offer a memorable outdoor adventure for all skill levels.

title: Farm Tour at The Ecology Center in San Juan Capistrano
description: The Ecology Center offers hands-on farm tours where kids can meet chickens, goats, and other farm animals while learning about sustainable agriculture. Families stroll through organic gardens and explore composting, beekeeping, and farm-to-table practices in a beautiful outdoor setting. It's an educational and engaging experience that connects children with where their food comes from.
address: 32701 Alipaz St, San Juan Capistrano, CA 92675

title: Sensory Playtime at San Clemente Library
description: Little ones can explore different textures, sounds, and sensory experiences in a safe, playful environment at San Clemente Library. This program offers hands-on activities designed to stimulate young minds and support early development. Perfect for toddlers who learn best through touch and exploration.
address: 242 Avenida Del Mar, San Clemente, CA 92672

title: Toddler Craft Storytime in Huntington Beach
description: This special storytime combines reading with simple crafts designed just for toddlers. After listening to engaging stories, little ones can create age-appropriate art projects that reinforce the themes of the books. It's a delightful blend of literacy and creativity tailored for the youngest library visitors.

title: Family Board Games at Foothill Ranch Library
description: Families can gather around the table for friendly competition and classic board game fun at Foothill Ranch Library. With a variety of games available for different ages and skill levels, everyone can join in. It's a screen-free way to connect, laugh, and create family memories.
address: 27002 Ridge Route Drive, Foothill Ranch, CA 92610

title: The Science of Spin: Preschool STEM at Library of the Canyons Library
description: Preschoolers can discover the wonders of motion and physics through hands-on experiments at this STEM-focused program. Young scientists will explore how things spin, rotate, and move through playful activities designed for little learners. A fantastic introduction to science concepts in an age-appropriate, engaging way.
address: 19721 Ridge Park Drive, Santa Clarita, CA 91351

title: Toy Time at Stanton Library
description: Little ones can explore, play, and socialize during this open play session at Stanton Library. It's a perfect opportunity for toddlers and preschoolers to enjoy toys and games in a welcoming library setting.

title: Weekend Walks Family Program in Anaheim ($5 per family) Registration Required
description: This guided nature walk takes families through Anaheim's scenic trails while learning about local plants, wildlife, and natural history. A naturalist or park ranger leads the exploration, making it educational and fun for all ages. Registration is required, and the $5 per family fee makes it an affordable weekend adventure.
address: 6700 E Walnut Canyon Rd, Anaheim, CA 92807, USA

title: Bilingual Japanese Storytime ⽇本語の読み聞かせ at Westminster Library
description: Children can experience stories in both Japanese and English during this special bilingual storytime. The program introduces young learners to Japanese language and culture through engaging tales and interactive reading.

title: Play and Learn at Costa Mesa Donald Dungan Library
description: Toddlers and preschoolers can engage in hands-on learning activities designed to build early literacy and social skills. This interactive program combines play with educational fun in a library environment.

title: Morning Kids' Skate Session in Orange
description: Kids can practice their roller skating skills during this morning session designed especially for young skaters. The rink offers a safe, fun environment with music and open space to glide, practice tricks, or just enjoy time with friends. It's a great way to get active and burn energy before lunchtime.

title: Crafty Saturday: Chia Seed Terrariums at San Juan Capistrano Library
description: Kids can get their hands dirty creating their own miniature chia seed terrariums to take home and watch grow. This weekend craft workshop combines gardening and creativity in a fun, interactive project.

title: Barnes and Noble Storytime at Bella Terra Huntington Beach
description: Barnes & Noble at Bella Terra invites young readers to their weekly storytime featuring popular children's books and interactive activities. Kids can sit on the story rug and listen to engaging tales read by enthusiastic booksellers, followed by related crafts or playtime. It's a relaxed bookstore event that celebrates reading in a casual atmosphere.

title: Play & Learn at Westminster Library
description: Young children can explore educational toys, games, and activities that promote early development and learning. This drop-in playtime session offers a fun, interactive environment for littles to socialize and discover.

title: Arts in Irvine Family Event
description: Families can explore various art activities and creative projects at this community arts event in Irvine. Kids have the opportunity to try different art mediums, participate in workshops, and express their creativity in a supportive environment. It's a wonderful chance to discover new artistic talents and enjoy making art together.
address: 1 Irvine Park Rd, Orange, CA 92869, USA

title: Open Aquarium Hours at Orange Coast College
description: Orange Coast College opens its teaching aquarium to the public, allowing families to view colorful marine life native to California waters. Kids can observe fish, invertebrates, and other sea creatures up close while learning about ocean ecosystems from student docents. It's a hidden gem that offers an educational peek into marine biology.

title: Family Movie at La Palma Library
description: Families can settle in for a movie screening at the library, perfect for a relaxing Saturday afternoon together. Bring blankets and get comfortable for this community film event.

title: Saturday - Open Sewing Lab For Teens and Adults at Laguna Niguel Library
description: Teens and adults can practice their sewing skills or learn new techniques during this open lab session. Bring your own project and use the library's sewing machines and supplies to create something special.

title: Tiny Terrariums at Ladera Ranch Library
description: Kids can craft their own miniature ecosystems in this hands-on terrarium workshop. They'll learn about plants while creating a beautiful tiny garden to bring home and care for.

title: Family Art Experience at Laguna Beach Library
description: Families can explore their creativity together through hands-on art activities and projects. This interactive art session welcomes all skill levels and provides materials for making memorable creations.

title: Instant Spicy Ramen Challenge at Fountain Valley Library
description: Brave teens can test their taste buds in this fun and fiery ramen eating challenge. See who can handle the heat in this lighthearted competition that brings friends together.

title: Jr. Ranger Adventure at Clark Regional Park
description: Children can become Junior Rangers through outdoor activities, nature games, and wildlife education at Clark Regional Park. This OC Parks program teaches kids about local ecosystems, conservation, and park stewardship in a fun, interactive way. Participants complete challenges to earn their official Junior Ranger badge.

title: Crafternoon: Paint A Birdhouse at Stanton Library
description: Kids can paint and personalize their own wooden birdhouse to take home and hang in the yard. This crafternoon session combines creativity with nature appreciation in a fun afternoon project.

title: Lego Club at Laguna Beach Library
description: Young builders can bring their imagination to life with unlimited LEGO bricks and building challenges. This club provides a creative space for kids to construct, share ideas, and show off their creations.

title: Teen Craft: Mini Bookshelf at Westminster Library
description: Teens can build their own miniature bookshelf in this hands-on woodworking craft session. It's a practical project that results in a functional piece perfect for displaying small books or collectibles.

title: Family Skate Night at Fountain Valley Skating Center ($40 for family of up to 5)
description: The Fountain Valley Skating Center hosts a special family skate night where families of up to five can enjoy roller skating together for one flat fee. The evening includes music, lights, and a fun atmosphere perfect for making family memories on wheels. Skate rentals are included, making it easy for everyone to join in.

The festival celebrates the state's culturally rich flavors with food marketplace booths, special experiences, and family-friendly tastings throughout Disney California Adventure Park. Park admission is required, with individual food items and select experiences available for purchase.
price: Included with admission: food items available for purchase; some experiences require additional fee

title: Egg’Straordinary  Annual  Pancake Breakfast & Egg Hunt
description: The City of San Gabriel's 81st annual spring celebration combines a pancake breakfast ($5 per plate), free egg hunts for kids 12 and under, and lively pancake races for ages 7-16. Families can enjoy this multi-generational tradition at Smith Park with activities for all ages. Pancake racing is encouraged, but eating the evidence is probably the best part!
price: Egg hunt: Free; Pancake breakfast: $5 per plate
ageRange: All ages; egg hunt for 12 and under; Pancake races for ages 7 - 16

title: Spring Egg Hunt & Healthy Kids Day
description: Recreation Park transforms into a spring wonderland with free age-specific egg hunts, arts and crafts, face painting, music, and photos with the Spring Bunny. Activities cater to everyone from toddlers to teens, making it a perfect family outing for kids ages 0-16. This annual event in Monrovia combines healthy active fun with seasonal celebration.

title: Splash N' Dash Egg Hunt at the Aquatic Center
description: Grab your swimsuit for an egg hunt with a splash! Kids ages 3-10 can dive into the waterslide pool at the Santa Clarita Aquatic Center to hunt for eggs in a unique twist on the traditional Easter activity. Bring food donations for the local food pantry to support the community.
address: 20850 Centre Pointe Parkway, Santa Clarita, CA 91350

description: Dive into the magical world of Hayao Miyazaki's beloved film about a goldfish and her human friend. This enchanting exhibition features over 100 original materials including art boards, posters, hand drawings by Miyazaki himself, and an authentic Studio Ghibli animation desk, plus special screenings and family workshops.

title: Mummies of the World at the California Science Center
description: This fascinating exhibition returns with an all-new collection of naturally and intentionally preserved human and animal mummies from South America, Europe, and ancient Egypt. Families can explore the science behind mummification through over 30 remarkable specimens never before seen in Los Angeles.
address: 700 Exposition Park Dr, Los Angeles, CA 90037

title: Eggstravaganza 2026 at Disneyland Resort
description: This interactive Disney-themed egg hunt transforms the park into a treasure map adventure. Families pick up a special guide and search for hidden eggs featuring Disney character faces throughout the park, then collect stickers and claim a prize when the map is complete—perfect for regular visitors looking for a fresh park experience.

title: Butterfly Pavilion at the NHM
description: The seasonal Butterfly Pavilion invites families to walk among hundreds of live butterflies in the museum's beautiful Nature Gardens. It's a peaceful, magical experience where kids can observe these delicate creatures up close as they flutter and feed all around them.

title: LA County Fair
description: The beloved LA County Fair is back with a "Play Your Way" theme, offering families cooler May weather to enjoy carnival rides, farm animals, live entertainment, and all the fair food favorites. This annual tradition brings together games, exhibits, and fun for the whole family at the Fairplex.

title: Free Day at The Autry
description: The Autry celebrates the art, history, and cultures of the American West with free afternoon admission every Tuesday and Wednesday from 1pm to 4pm. Families can explore galleries filled with Western artifacts, Native American art, and interactive exhibits that bring history to life.
locationName: The Autry Museum of the American West
address: 4700 Western Heritage Way, Los Angeles, CA 90027

title: Game On!  at the California Science Center
description: This dynamic exhibition explores the science behind sports and play, celebrating how the human body moves and performs. Families can discover the physics, biology, and joy of athletic movement through hands-on activities that get everyone up and moving.
address: 700 Exposition Park Dr, Los Angeles, CA 90037

title: Jaws: The Exhibition
description: This immersive exhibition dives deep into Steven Spielberg's iconic 1975 blockbuster, exploring the filmmaking magic behind one of cinema's most thrilling movies. The Academy Museum presents behind-the-scenes artifacts, props, and stories that reveal how this groundbreaking film changed Hollywood forever.
city: The Academy Museum of Motion Pictures
price: Included with admission; (Visitors 17 and under get in free)

title: Pre-Natal Breastfeeding Workshop
description: Expectant parents can prepare for their breastfeeding journey with this comprehensive workshop covering everything from the first latch to troubleshooting common challenges. The class provides practical tips and answers questions to help new families feel confident and supported.
